title: Draft Regulation of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a carbon border adjustment mechanism (CBAM)
bodyHtml:
The Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) of the EU was announced as a part of the "Fit for 55" package, which aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by at least 55 percent by 2030 compared to 1990 levels, in line with the European Climate Law. Currently, the EU applies a carbon pricing mechanism under the Emission Trading System (ETS) to decarbonize its industry. To maintain the competitiveness of Europe and prevent carbon leakage, which may cause production to shift to countries with lower emission reduction targets than the EU, a carbon tax will be applied to certain products imported to the EU with the "Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)".

The political agreement on the CBAM proposal presented by the European Commission on July 14, 2021, was reached between the European Parliament (EP) and the EU Council on December 13, 2022, within the scope of the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism. The agreed CBAM text was published by the EU Council, and the legal review process for this text is ongoing. After the review process, it is expected that the CBAM Regulation will be translated into the official languages of the EU and published.

In this context, the final draft of the CBAM Regulation, which was translated into Turkish by AKIB along with a brief information note about the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism to prevent carbon leakage and protect the competitiveness of producers in the relevant sectors, is shared in the  below links.

Please click for the information note and the unofficial translation.
